title: Lesson 25: Mini Project — Build Your First Multi-Page Website

content.explanation:
🚀 You Are Now Building Real Websites

Up to this point, you have learned how individual HTML pieces work.

Now it is time to combine everything into a real project.

🌐 What You Will Build

  • A homepage (index.html)
  • An about page (about.html)
  • A contact section (contact.html structure)
  • Navigation links between pages
  • Images and structured content

⚡ Developer Goal

👉 Build a complete website like a real developer would on GitHub

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Project Structure Example

index.html
about.html
contact.html
images/
  profile.jpg

🔗 Navigation Example

<a href="index.html">Home</a>
<a href="about.html">About</a>
<a href="contact.html">Contact</a>

🧠 Key Idea

👉 Websites are just connected HTML pages working together

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My First Website

Welcome to my personal project site.

mini_challenge.instruction: 🎯 FINAL PROJECT MISSION: Build a full homepage that includes: 1. A main heading (h1) 2. A short introduction paragraph 3. A navigation menu with 3 links: - Home (index.html) - About (about.html) - Contact (contact.html) 4. Add one image using an tag 5. Add a second paragraph describing your website 🔥 Combine everything you have learned so far
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Structure example:

My Website

About

Description here

quiz_engine.question: What is the main goal of this mini project?
options: To build a complete multi-page website using everything learned so far
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 This project combines all your HTML skills into a real website structure similar to what developers build and deploy.

takeaways: Real websites combine multiple HTML pages
Images and text create complete user experiences
Project thinking is more important than memorizing tags
This is your first real developer-style website

cheat_sheet.website_structure: Multiple HTML files connected via links
cheat_sheet.rule: Think in pages, not just tags
